title: TSMC adds $100 billion to Arizona investment amid AI chip demand
excerpt: The fresh commitment raises TSMC's total planned spending in Arizona to $265 billion, covering new wafer fabs and advanced packaging facilities
content_html:
Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. is committing an additional $100 billion to its Arizona operations, citing what the company describes as a multi-year structural demand surge for AI chips. The pledge brings TSMC's total planned investment in Arizona to $265 billion.

Chief Financial Officer Wendell Huang said the new funds will cover both front-end wafer fabrication facilities and back-end advanced packaging facilities. The company also raised its full-year capital expenditure guidance to between $60 billion and $64 billion, the company said.

"We're seeing this strong-structure, multi-year demand, and we do not plan to leave any food on the table for anybody else," Huang told CNBC. "As long as the megatrend is right, then we're able to continue to deliver the profitable growth to our shareholders."

TSMC's first Arizona fabrication plant is up and running, with yields matching those of its primary Taiwan facility, according to Reuters. Equipment installation at a second fab is imminent, a third fab is currently under construction, and groundwork for both a fourth fab and the site's inaugural advanced packaging facility is already in motion. Taken together, existing and future projects are expected to grow TSMC's Arizona presence to 12 fabrication and advanced packaging facilities alongside a dedicated R&D center.

Wendell Huang acknowledged physical constraints facing the expansion, including a shortage of construction workers and infrastructure limitations in Arizona, and said the company would work with government officials to address them.

On chip technology, TSMC is converting 5-nanometer capacity to the 3-nanometer node to meet customer demand, Huang told CNBC. He described 2-nanometer technology as the company's newest revenue driver heading into the third quarter, following its initial revenue generation in the second quarter. The smaller the transistor, the more that can be packed onto a single chip, yielding more powerful and efficient semiconductors.

TSMC's second-quarter results, which the company reported last week alongside the Arizona investment announcement, showed net income of NT$706.56 billion, a 77.4% increase from the same period a year earlier and a record for the fifth consecutive quarter. High-performance computing — the category that includes AI chips — generated 66% of second-quarter revenue. For the third quarter, TSMC forecast revenue of between $44.6 billion and $45.8 billion, the company said.

TSMC stock fell 7.3% on Friday despite the record results but remains up roughly 48% this year.

sentences.zh: 尽管业绩创纪录，台积电股价周五仍下跌 7.3%，但今年仍上涨约 48%。

content_html_zh:
台积电 该公司将向其亚利桑那州业务追加 1000 亿美元，理由是该公司称 AI 芯片的多年结构性需求激增。 这一承诺使台积电在亚利桑那州的计划投资总额达到 2650 亿美元。

首席财务官黄文德尔表示，新资金将覆盖前端晶圆制造设施和后端先进封装设施。 该公司表示，该公司还将全年资本支出指导上调至 600 亿至 640 亿美元。

黄告诉 CNBC：“我们看到了这种强劲的结构性、多年需求，我们不打算为其他任何人留下任何食物。” “只要大趋势是正确的，我们就能够继续为股东带来利润增长。”

据路透社报道，台积电在亚利桑那州的第一家制造工厂已建成并投入运营，产量与其在台湾的主要工厂相当。 第二座晶圆厂的设备安装即将进行，第三座晶圆厂目前正在建设中，第四座晶圆厂和该基地首座先进封装设施的基础工作已经启动。 总而言之，现有和未来的项目预计将使台积电在亚利桑那州的业务增加到 12 个制造和先进封装设施以及一个专门的研发中心。

Wendell Huang承认扩张面临物理限制，包括建筑工人短缺和亚利桑那州基础设施限制，并表示该公司将与政府官员合作解决这些问题。

黄告诉 CNBC，在芯片技术方面，台积电正在将 5 纳米产能转换为 3 纳米节点，以满足客户需求。 他将 2 纳米技术描述为继第二季度首次产生收入之后进入第三季度的最新收入驱动因素。 晶体管越小，单个芯片上可以封装的晶体管就越多，从而产生更强大、更高效的半导体。

台积电上周与亚利桑那州投资公告一起公布的第二季度业绩显示，净利润为新台币 7065.6 亿元，同比增长 77.4%，连续第五个季度创历史新高。 高性能计算——包括人工智能芯片在内的类别——创造了第二季度收入的 66%。 台积电表示，预计第三季度营收将在 446 亿美元至 458 亿美元之间。

尽管业绩创纪录，台积电股价周五仍下跌 7.3%，但今年仍上涨约 48%。
